zh.Atherton, Lancashire, England, United Ki: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾阿瑟頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Jane or Joan Moberley於大約1323年出生在英國爾莫波利時，她的父親William Mobberly41歲，母親Maud Downes45歲。她於1344年與Sir William de Atherton I, Knight在英國爾莫波利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1375年在英國爾享年54歲。

zh-hans.Atherton, Lancashire, England, United Ki: 英国英格兰藍卡爾阿瑟頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Jane or Joan Moberley于大约1323年出生在英国爾莫波利时，她的父亲William Mobberly41岁，母亲Maud Downes45岁。她于1344年与Sir William de Atherton I, Knight在英国爾莫波利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1375年在英国爾享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moberley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Worcestershire and Hampshire): variant of   Moberly  .

variants.name: Moberly
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from Mobberley in Cheshire. The placename derives from Old English mōt ‘meeting’ + burg ‘fortress’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Jane or Joan Moberley was born about 1323, in Mobberley, Cheshire, England, her father, William Mobberly, was 41 and her mother, Maud Downes, was 45. She married Sir William de Atherton I, Knight in 1344, in Mobberley, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1375, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Jane or Joan Moberley est née environ 1323, à Mobberley, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, William Mobberly, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Maud Downes, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de Atherton I, Knight en 1344, à Mobberley, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1375, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Jane or Joan Moberley um 1323 in Mobberley, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Mobberly 41 und ihre Mutter Maud Downes 45. Sie hat 1344 in Mobberley, Cheshire, England Sir William de Atherton I, Knight geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1375 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Jane or Joan Moberley è nata il circa 1323 nel Mobberley, Cheshire, England, sua padre, William Mobberly, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Maud Downes, aveva 45 anni. Lei sposato Sir William de Atherton I, Knight nel 1344, nel Mobberley, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1375, nel Cheshire, England, all'età di 54 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jane or Joan Moberley(이)가 대략 1323년 영국 체실르 모브벌리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Mobberly(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Downes(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1344년 영국 체실르 모브벌리에서 Sir William de Atherton I, Knight(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1375년 영국 체실르에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Sir William de Atherton I, Knight em 1344, em Mobberley, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Jane or Joan Moberley родилась около 1323 г. в местности моббирли, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Mobberly было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Maud Downes, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Atherton I, Knight в 1344 г. в местности моббирли, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1375 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 54 лет.
